fplGuidelines.source: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
fplGuidelines.note: For the 48 contiguous states and DC. Alaska and Hawaii have higher thresholds.

section501r.summary: Requires tax-exempt hospitals to establish a Financial Assistance Policy (FAP), make reasonable efforts to determine FAP eligibility before extraordinary collection actions, limit charges to FAP-eligible patients, and bill FAP-eligible patients no more than amounts generally billed to insured patients.
keyProvisions: Hospitals must have a written Financial Assistance Policy
FAP must be widely publicized and available in the community
Hospitals must make reasonable efforts to determine eligibility before sending bills to collections
FAP-eligible patients cannot be charged more than amounts generally billed to insured patients
Hospitals cannot use extraordinary collection actions (liens, garnishment, lawsuits) without first making reasonable efforts to determine FAP eligibility

fdcpa.summary: Prohibits abusive, unfair, and deceptive practices by third-party debt collectors. Gives consumers the right to demand debt validation within 30 days of receiving written notice from the collector, during which collection activity must cease.
keyProvisions: Collectors must send written notice of the debt within 5 days of first contact
Consumers have 30 days to dispute the debt in writing
During the validation period, collector must cease collection activity
Collector must provide verification of the debt if disputed
Collectors cannot call before 8am or after 9pm
Collectors cannot use threats, harassment, or deceptive practices
Collectors cannot contact consumers at work if told not to

validationRights.description: Consumer must send written dispute within 30 days of receiving initial notice. After 30 days, consumer can still dispute but collector is not required to stop collection during verification.

noSurprisesAct.statute: Public Law 116-260, Division BB, Title I
noSurprisesAct.shortName: No Surprises Act — Good Faith Estimate Protections
noSurprisesAct.summary: Requires healthcare providers to give uninsured/self-pay patients a Good Faith Estimate (GFE) of expected charges. If the actual bill exceeds the GFE by $400 or more, patients can initiate a Patient-Provider Dispute Resolution (PPDR) process.
keyProvisions: Providers must give GFE to uninsured/self-pay patients upon scheduling or request
GFE must be provided at least 1 business day before scheduled service (3 days for services scheduled 10+ days out)
If actual charges exceed GFE by $400 or more, patient can initiate PPDR
PPDR must be initiated within 120 calendar days of receiving the bill
Provider must not pursue collection while PPDR is pending
Independent dispute resolution entity makes binding determination

hipaaPatientRights.summary: Patients have the right to access their medical records and billing information, including itemized statements of charges.
